@graph.description:
We want to recreate the Corces et al. 2016 Nat Genet Fast-ATAC hematopoiesis atlas. The data is on GEO GSE74912 (bulk ATAC-seq + RNA-seq) and GSE74310 (single-cell ATAC subset). The cohort is 9 healthy donors and 12 AML patients across 13 FACS-sorted populations: HSC, MPP, LMPP, CMP, GMP, MEP, CLP (7 stem/progenitor) plus erythroblast, B, CD4 T, CD8 T, NK, monocyte (6 differentiated). 3-4 adult donors per population yield 49 transcriptomes and 77 regulomes; AML samples add ~11 additional ATAC-seq libraries.

Process Fast-ATAC reads with cutadapt for adapter trimming, Bowtie2 v2.x with --very-sensitive -X 2000 against hg19, Picard MarkDuplicates with duplicates removed, MAPQ >= 30, mitochondrial reads removed, ENCODE blacklist subtracted, and Tn5 read shift +4/-5. Call peaks with MACS2 narrow --nomodel --shift -75 --extsize 150 -p 0.01 --call-summits.

Build a master peak union by merging peaks present in >= 2 of 3+ donors per population. The expected union is ~590,650 accessible peaks. Compute per-replicate Pearson R within and across donors; headline numbers to reproduce: technical R = 0.98 within HSCs, biological R = 0.97; mean technical R = 0.94 across all populations.

For enhancer cytometry, use CIBERSORT trained on cell-type-specific regulatory elements; leave-one-out cross-validation should classify at least 11 of 13 populations (HSC vs MPP is the expected difficult pair). Apply on bulk CD34+ HSPC samples and compare to flow cytometry: target R^2 = 0.95.

For TF motif analysis, cluster JASPAR motifs into a non-redundant set of 46 hematopoiesis TF motifs (Fig 4a). Correlate per-cell-type motif accessibility with TF expression from RNA-seq. Reproduce GATA1 <-> GATA motif R = 0.73 at p = 1e-18 and PAX5 <-> PAX motif R = 0.88 at p = 1e-230. Run GATA footprint analysis on MEPs vs CLPs and confirm no GATA footprint in CLPs.

Overlap accessibility with GWAS lead SNPs and recover per-trait cell-type enrichment: mean corpuscular volume -> erythroblast, rheumatoid arthritis -> lymphoid, Alzheimer's -> microglia/monocyte.

For the AML arm, profile pre-leukemic mutation burden in HSC, T, blast cells across 39 AML patients (driver-mutation panel from Suppl Table 5). Project AML cell types onto the first 4 PCs of normal hematopoiesis and reproduce: LSCs near GMP/LMPP; blasts spread along GMP-monocyte axis.

Reference genome is hg19. No mechanistic claims -- descriptive regulome atlas + AML evolution trajectory.
@graph.name:
computational biology — ATAC-seq accessible-region calling. Standard pipeline: raw QC with
insert-size distribution check, optional Tn5-aware trimming, alignment
with soft-clipping, MACS3 BAMPE peak calling (--shift -75 --extsize 150),
TSS-enrichment validation, and HOMER/MEME motif enrichment on
accessible-region centers. Mirrors `config/stage-taxonomies/atac-seq.yaml`.

@graph.description: The domain expert who resolved discovery decisions during intake chat, prior to agent execution.
@graph.name: Subject Matter Expert (intake)
Agent Instructions
Workflow Context
Discovery and scoring policies
Execution provenance log
Homo sapiens
Discover method for sequence_trimming (options: trimmomatic, cutadapt, fastp, trim_galore). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for peak_calling (options: macs2, macs3, seacr, genrich). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for alignment (options: star, hisat2, bwa_mem2, bwa_mem, minimap2, bowtie2). Surface set_intake_method.

Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Validate outputs of: Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Validate outputs of: Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Validate outputs of: Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Validate outputs of: Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Identify regions of significant read enrichment from ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq /
CUT&RUN alignments. MACS2 / MACS3 / SEACR / Genrich selected at runtime
by mark type (narrow / broad).

Validate outputs of: Identify regions of significant read enrichment from ChIP-seq / ATAC-seq /
CUT&RUN alignments. MACS2 / MACS3 / SEACR / Genrich selected at runtime
by mark type (narrow / broad).

Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Validate outputs of: Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.

Validate outputs of: Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.
